introSection: Road Runner Express is a Vekoma kiddie roller coaster located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA. It was moved from the former Six Flags New Orleans, and opened to the public on May 28, 2011.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster opened as Rex's Rail Runner with Jazzland in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA on May 20, 2000. It was located in the Kid's Carnival children's area. For 2003, it was renamed Road Runner's Express when the park was rebranded as Six Flags New Orleans, with the children's area being renamed to Looney Tunes Adventures with the introduction of the Warner Bros. licensing.

It operated until Hurricane Katrina hit the park in August 2005. After severe flooding, Six Flags New Orleans never reopened and the coaster was dismantled in 2009. The coaster was then shipped by Six Flags to Larson International in Plainview, Texas for refurbishment.

After refurbishment, the coaster was later moved to Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California. The coaster was teased in November 2009. In January 2010, it was announced as Mr. Six's Dance Coaster and was supposed to open in the Cyclone Bay area of the park that year near Déjà Vu.

After several delays and due to budget cutbacks, the opening date was moved back to mid-2011 and its construction site was instead moved to the Bugs Bunny World children's section of the park in December 2010, where Yosemite Sam Sierra Falls previously stood. A DC Comics theme was later considered, known as Little Flash, which was eventually dropped in favor of its original theme. It reopened on May 28, 2011.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Road Runner Express currently has red track and black supports. While at Six Flags New Orleans it initially had blue track and supports and was later repainted red with orange supports.

fullText:
Road Runner Express is a Vekoma kiddie roller coaster located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA. It was moved from the former Six Flags New Orleans, and opened to the public on May 28, 2011.

The roller coaster opened as Rex's Rail Runner with Jazzland in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA on May 20, 2000. It was located in the Kid's Carnival children's area. For 2003, it was renamed Road Runner's Express when the park was rebranded as Six Flags New Orleans, with the children's area being renamed to Looney Tunes Adventures with the introduction of the Warner Bros. licensing.

It operated until Hurricane Katrina hit the park in August 2005. After severe flooding, Six Flags New Orleans never reopened and the coaster was dismantled in 2009. The coaster was then shipped by Six Flags to Larson International in Plainview, Texas for refurbishment.

After refurbishment, the coaster was later moved to Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California. The coaster was teased in November 2009. In January 2010, it was announced as Mr. Six's Dance Coaster and was supposed to open in the Cyclone Bay area of the park that year near Déjà Vu.

After several delays and due to budget cutbacks, the opening date was moved back to mid-2011 and its construction site was instead moved to the Bugs Bunny World children's section of the park in December 2010, where Yosemite Sam Sierra Falls previously stood. A DC Comics theme was later considered, known as Little Flash, which was eventually dropped in favor of its original theme. It reopened on May 28, 2011.



Road Runner Express currently has red track and black supports. While at Six Flags New Orleans it initially had blue track and supports and was later repainted red with orange supports.
